segments.text: My son, do not forget my law, But let your heart keep my commands;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: For length of days and long life And peace they will add to you.
Let not mercy and truth forsake you; Bind them around your neck, Write them on the tablet of your heart,
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: And so find favor and high esteem In the sight of God and man.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart, And lean not on your own understanding;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: In all your ways acknowledge Him, And He shall direct your paths.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear the Lord and depart from evil.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: It will be health to your flesh, And strength to your bones.
Honor the Lord with your possessions, And with the firstfruits of all your increase;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: So your barns will be filled with plenty, And your vats will overflow with new wine.
My son, do not despise the chastening of the Lord, Nor detest His correction;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: For whom the Lord loves He corrects, Just as a father the son in whom he delights.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Happy is the man who finds wisdom, And the man who gains understanding;
For her proceeds are better than the profits of silver, And her gain than fine gold.
She is more precious than rubies, And all the things you may desire cannot compare with her.
Length of days is in her right hand, In her left hand riches and honor.
Her ways are ways of pleasantness, And all her paths are peace.
She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her, And happy are all who retain her.
The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; By understanding He established the heavens;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: By His knowledge the depths were broken up, And clouds drop down the dew.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: My son, let them not depart from your eyes— Keep sound wisdom and discretion;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: So they will be life to your soul And grace to your neck.
Then you will walk safely in your way, And your foot will not stumble.
When you lie down, you will not be afraid; Yes, you will lie down and your sleep will be sweet.
Do not be afraid of sudden terror, Nor of trouble from the wicked when it comes;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: For the Lord will be your confidence, And will keep your foot from being caught.
Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, When it is in the power of your hand to do so.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: Do not say to your neighbor, 'Go, and come back, And tomorrow I will give it,' When you have it with you.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: Do not devise evil against your neighbor, For he dwells by you for safety's sake.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: Do not strive with a man without cause, If he has done you no harm.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: Do not envy the oppressor, And choose none of his ways;
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Solomon

segments.text: For the perverse person is an abomination to the Lord, But His secret counsel is with the upright.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The curse of the Lord is on the house of the wicked, But He blesses the home of the just.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Surely He scorns the scornful, But gives grace to the humble.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The wise shall inherit glory, But shame shall be the legacy of fools.
